title: swift-json Experiments
blurb: Experiments grounded in swift-json's parsing API.

experiments.purpose: Standing parse-performance + tree-equivalence harness for swift-json. Two complementary modes: (1) wall-clock benches comparing `JSON.parse(String)` / `JSON.parse([UInt8])` against `Foundation.JSONSerialization` and against pure byte-iteration floors; (2) `sanity` / `equiv` modes that prove both parsers produce structurally-equivalent trees (top-level keys, symbols.count, relationships.count, module.name, symbols[0].kind.identifier, relationships[0].kind, deep pathComponents — all match). Used as the measurement gate for Tiers 0/1/3/4 of the parse-performance arc.
experiments.statusDetail: Last reading on 86 MB Swift.symbols.json, release, macOS 26 arm64, 3-iter average: Foundation.JSONSerialization 0.299s; swift-json JSON.parse([UInt8]) 0.304s (1.02× Foundation); swift-json JSON.parse(String) 0.316s (1.06× Foundation). Tree equivalence verified across 7 fields (counts, top-level keys, kind discriminators, deep pathComponents). Used as the empirical gate for `parse-performance.md` v1.2.0 DECISION and `parse-performance-architecture.md` v1.0.2 DECISION.

experiments.purpose: Phase A0 feasibility spike for `swift-foundations/swift-json/Research/parse-performance-architecture.md` v1.0.0 (Tier 4 Span-cursor design). Two independent compiler-binding checks: (1) `check-contiguous-storage` verifies `String.UTF8View.withContiguousStorageIfAvailable` engagement across String shapes; (2) `check-span-typed-throws` verifies `Span<UInt8>` + `throws(E)` + `@_lifetime(borrow bytes)` composition for the proposed `RFC_8259.Lexer.Span` cursor shape.
experiments.statusDetail: All three Phase A0 premises GREEN. (1) `~Escapable` on associated types: user-confirmed via `.enableExperimentalFeature("SuppressedAssociatedTypes")` (originally CONFIRMED 2026-02-13 in `swift-parser-primitives/Experiments/suppressed-escapable-associated-types/`). (2) `String.UTF8View.withContiguousStorageIfAvailable` fired on all 7 probed shapes including bridged NSString (more favourable than the architecture doc's projection). (3) `Span<UInt8>` + typed throws + `@_lifetime(borrow bytes)` compiles and runs; typed errors propagate across `inout Cursor` boundaries; `~Escapable` enforcement holds. Phase A1 (Span lexer + parser implementation) is unblocked.

experiments.purpose: Verify that `swift-symbolgraph-extract` emits sufficient `conformsTo` relationships in its JSON output to reconstruct a protocol-refinement table — replacing the hardcoded `idiomKnownStdlibRefinements` in `Lint.Rule.Idiom.RedundantRefinement` with a precomputed oracle. The empirical foundation for Shape F in `swift-foundations/swift-linter/Research/lsp-sourcekit-integration.md` v1.1.0.
experiments.statusDetail: All three hypothesis claims dispositioned: (1) protocol-protocol conformsTo IS in symbol-graph JSON; (2) ~120-line reducer suffices; (3) wall-clock under 30s CONFIRMED for per-institute-module graphs (Carrier 48ms), REFUTED for Swift stdlib graph (128s) — swift-json text-parser bottleneck on 86 MB pretty-printed input, not a format limitation; production oracle generation is offline. ALL 26 entries of the hardcoded `idiomKnownStdlibRefinements` table reproduced; 110 additional refinements extracted.
